wikipediaRefs.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
wikipediaRefs.snippet: CDC is described as the national public health agency of the United States and a federal agency under HHS, headquartered in Atlanta; it is also noted to have faced major disruptions during the second Trump administration.

wikipediaRefs.title: Vaccine
wikipediaRefs.snippet: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious or malignant disease.

wikipediaRefs.title: United States

claims.postExcerpt: Massachusetts Public Health Commissioner Dr. Robbie Goldstein is a Tufts University-trained physician who’s spent his entire career as an infectious disease specialist. He’s on the infectious disease faculty at MGH and teaches at Harvard Medical School. He did a two-year tour at the Centers for Dise

sources.title: Massachusetts health chief: Vaccine disinformation fight takes 90% of job
sources.snippet: “less than half of all Americans now saying they have confidence” (May tracking poll by KFF)

sources.title: CDC panel overturns 1991 newborn vaccine policy, triggering warnings of a public health backslide
sources.snippet: Hepatitis B shot has been standard practice for newborns since 1991.

sources.title: In RFK Jr.’s upside-down world of vaccines, panel votes to end hepatitis B shot at birth
sources.snippet: Says ending hepatitis B recommendation could reverse progress; mentions reducing childhood infections by 99%.

sources.title: CDC Vaccine Databases, Crucial For Managing Outbreaks, Are Out Of Date
sources.snippet: Nearly half of CDC vaccine databases paused without explanation; ~90% of stale databases vaccine-related
